title: ExecutionProof
description: The cryptographic boundary artefact verified by the protected resource. Authorises ONE exact (action, target, parameters) tuple under the resource owner's configured trust policy. Protocol version 1.0.0.

proof_id.description: The unique identifier of this proof. MUST use the 'proof_' prefix.

issuer.description: The issuer of this proof (the authority broker or its delegated signer).

subject.description: The principal (agent, user, or service) on whose behalf the proof was issued.

audience.description: The intended audience (verifier service) for this proof. The verifier MUST reject proofs minted for a different audience.

action.description: The exact action this proof authorises. The verifier MUST recompute the action_hash and compare.

target.description: The target resource this proof authorises the action against.

not_before.description: Optional. If present, the proof is not valid before this timestamp.

canonicalisation.description: The canonicalisation profile used to compute action_hash and signature. 'ACTENON-JCS-STRICT-1' is canonical for new proofs. 'RFC8785-JCS' is a deprecated alias for historical proofs. Any other value MUST be rejected with CANONICALISATION_FAILURE.

action_hash.description: The hash of the canonicalised (action, target, parameters) tuple. The verifier MUST recompute this and compare.

signature.description: The issuer's signature over the canonicalised proof payload.

execution_mode.description: The execution mode under which this proof was issued. MUST be carried through to the receipt and refusal.

grant_id.description: Optional. The authority broker's grant identifier under which this proof was issued. Used for revocation cascade.

authority_decision_id.description: Optional. The authority broker's decision record identifier. Used for audit.

evidence_links.description: Optional. Evidence links (transparency log entries, receipt chain entries, etc.) associated with this proof.

custom_claims.description: Optional. Implementation-specific custom claims. Consumers MUST ignore unknown claims. Custom claims MUST NOT override protocol-defined fields.
